source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the DH Zone Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the DH Zone Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the DH Zone Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within the DH zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Additional Built Form and Siting Requirements within the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Part V, Chapter 4, contains additional built form and siting

provisions.heading_context_raw: Part V, Chapter 4, contains additional built form and siting
provisions.text: Part V, Chapter 4, contains additional built form and siting requirements for the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall
provisions.text: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and
provisions.text: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and 132(3), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 0 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and
provisions.text: Within the Spring Garden Road (SGR) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, any portion of any main building above a height of 17.0 metres, measured at the streetline where the lot abuts Spring Garden Road, shall be required to be set back an additional 0.9 metre from the streetline for each additional 0.6 metre in building height above 17.0 metres (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 133 and Subsections 132(1.5), 132(2), and
provisions.text: Within the South Park Street (SPS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, any portion of any main building above a height of 17.0 metres, measured at the streetline where the lot abuts South Park Street, shall be required to be set back an additional 0.9 metre from the streetline for each additional 0.6 metre in building height above 17.0 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Underground Parking Exemption from Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Underground motor vehicle parking structures are exempt from the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Underground motor vehicle parking structures are exempt from the
provisions.text: Underground motor vehicle parking structures are exempt from the minimum required front or flanking setback requirements, but shall not protrude more than 0.25 metre above any streetline grade when located within required setbacks. Maximum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements for any main building located:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a lot containing a community recreation use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Any main building along a pedestrian-oriented commercial street, as shown on Schedule 7, shall have a maximum front or flanking setback, as specified on Schedule 19, unless Subsections 134(3), 134(4), or 134(5) apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Maximum front or flanking setbacks shall not apply to portions of lots that have a frontage less than 4.0 metres along a streetline or transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Where a transportation or utility easement abuts the streetline, a main building shall have a maximum front or flanking setback that is the greater of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the applicable distance as specified on Schedule 19; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the edge of the easement that is located farthest from the streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: The maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Subsection 134(2) may be exceeded, if an at-grade open space, such as a plaza, is located between a front or flanking lot line and a main building.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 134(7), if a maximum front or flanking setback is not specified on Schedule 19, the maximum required front or flanking setback shall be 4.0 metres. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: The maximum front or flanking setback may be exceeded, providing that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the setback is not exceeded by more than 50% of the façade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the requirements in subsection 420(7) are met. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Side Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and Section 427, the minimum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 4.5 metres from the side lot line abutting the lot; or (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: 0.0 metre elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum side setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any side yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Any main building on a lot that abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone shall be required to be setback above of the streetwall height no less than 6.0 metres from any side lot line. (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Any portion of any main building above the streetwall height, but less than 33.5 metres in height, shall be required to be set back from side lot lines of no less than 4.5 metres. This requirement may be reduced to 0.0 metre where:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: abutting lots are under common ownership and developed over a continuous foundation, footing, or underground parking structure; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: any main building is located within the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall have a minimum required setback from any side lot line of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: 6.0 metres, if the abutting property is within a DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, UC-2, or UC-1 zone and has a view plane restriction that would not permit the development of a high-rise building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: 11.5 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: The maximum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: on lots located within Downtown Halifax Central Blocks

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, 0.0 metre; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 134(3), 134(4), 134(5), and 135(2) and
provisions.text: on lots located outside of the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, 20% or less of the lot width. Rear Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum required rear setback for any main building shall be 0.0 metre.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 136(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, any main building shall be required to be set back no less than 4.5 metres from the rear lot line abutting the lot. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum rear setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any rear yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: A lot that abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone shall provide a minimum required setback above the streetwall height of 6.0 metres from any rear lot line. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Any portion of any main building above the streetwall height, but less than 33.5 metres in height, shall have a minimum required setback from a rear lot line of 4.5 metres. This requirement may be reduced to 0.0 metre where:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: abutting lots are under common ownership and developed over a continuous foundation, footing, or underground parking structure; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: any main building is located within the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall have a minimum required setback from any rear lot line of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 6.0 metres, if the abutting property is within a DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, UC-2, or UC-1 zone and has a view plane restriction that would not permit the development of a high-rise building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 136(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 11.5 metres elsewhere. Maximum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the maximum required streetwall heights for main buildings are specified on Schedule 20.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: On a site having sloping conditions, the maximum streetwall height required in Subsection 137(1), may be exceeded by:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: 10%, where any main building is located on a streetline or a transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that results in a slope that is between 4% and 8% across the entire width of the streetwall; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: 20%, where any main building is located on a streetline or a transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that results in a slope that exceeds 8% across the entire width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: On a registered heritage property, the maximum required streetwall height shall be the streetwall height of the registered heritage building on the coming into force date of this By-law.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 137(2), 137(3), and 137(4), the
provisions.text: The maximum required streetwall height may be exceeded by 1.5 metres to permit a clear glass guard and railing system or a parapet. Minimum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's minimum required streetwall height shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along pedestrian-oriented commercial streets, as shown on Schedule 7, 8.0 metres; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along all other streets or transportation reserves:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for any main building less than 8.0 metres in height, the building height, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 8.0 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Twenty percent or less of the entire streetwall width, to a maximum of 8.0 metres, may be reduced in height, providing the height is not reduced below 4.5 metres (Diagram 7).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 138(2) and 138(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Section 143, the minimum streetwall height required in Subsection 138(1) may be reduced to 3.5 metres along a streetline or a transportation reserve, if the slope exceeds 4% across the building width or building depth. (RC-May 23/24;E- June 13/24) Streetwall Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a minimum required streetwall stepback of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: 3.0 metres, for any portion of the main building less than 33.5 metres in height; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: 4.5 metres, for any portion of the main building greater than 33.5 metres in height.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for a maximum of 20% of the length of any main building facing each streetline or a transportation reserve provided that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: each section not having a streetwall stepback does not exceed 14.0 metres in length along the streetline or transportation reserve; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: there is a minimum required separation distance of 14.0 metres along the streetline or transportation reserve between sections not having a streetwall stepback. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 139(2), any main building shall have a
provisions.text: No portion of any main building above the streetwall shall project beyond the streetwall towards the front or flanking lot line. Streetwall Width Within the DH Zone

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor and subject to Subsections 140(2) and 140(3), a streetwall shall extend the full width of any streetline or transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: A streetwall located along Lower Water Street or Upper Water Street shall not have to extend the full width of the streetline or transportation reserve, if the lot:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: is located within both the Lower Centre Downtown Halifax

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: Special Area and the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: has a frontage greater than 27.5 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: The width of a streetwall may be reduced by a maximum of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: 20% of the lot width, for lots located outside of the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: 30% of the lot width, if an at-grade open space is provided, such as a plaza or a mid-block pedestrian connection; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any portion of a lot within a waterfront view corridor
provisions.text: 80% of the lot width, for cultural uses on lots located outside of the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, if an at-grade open space is provided, such as a plaza. (RCCC-Dec 09/24;E-Dec 24/24) Recessed Portions and Cantilevers within Streetwalls

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 141(2), the total combined width of all

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 141(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 141(2), the total combined width of all cantilevered and recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2.0 metres or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 141(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Cantilevered and recessed portions described in Subsection 141(1) shall include portals, but exclude balconies and recessed pedestrian entrances. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.
provisions.text: No maximum required lot coverage applies. Ground Floor Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, a ground floor shall be established along any streetline or transportation reserve. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: On a corner lot, for any streetwall along a streetline or transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that exceeds a slope greater than 8%, extended across the entire width of the streetwall, the requirements for a ground floor do not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: A ground floor shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 143(6), each ground floor required shall, for no less than 70% of each streetwall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 0.6 metre below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 143(5), commence no higher than 0.6 metre above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: In the event of a conflict between the coastal elevation requirements of Section 74 and Clause 143(4)(b), Clause 143(4)(b) shall not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor of a grade-related dwelling unit use shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: commence no higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 143(8) and excluding a low-density dwelling use, a grade-related dwelling unit use, or a residential lobby on a floor containing a grade-related dwelling unit use, the minimum required ground floor height shall be 4.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor height required in Subsection 143(7) may be reduced where one of the following conditions applies:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: the ground floor height of an addition is equal to or greater than the ground floor height of the existing main building;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: for a proposed main building on a registered heritage property, the ground floor heights of abutting main buildings along the same streetline are such that the required ground floor height of the proposed main building would be inconsistent with the abutting main buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: on a site located outside of the Central Blocks, as shown on Schedule 3B, and that is not along a pedestrian- oriented commercial street, as shown on Schedule 7, the ground floor height requirement may be reduced to 3.5 metres if the ground floor is to be fully occupied by residential uses.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 143(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Mezzanine spaces are permitted to commence within the minimum ground floor height required in Subsection 143(7), for those portions of a ground floor that are occupied by residential uses. Grade-Oriented Premises

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented premises shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, be located along any streetline; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E- Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: be required for the full width of the streetwall, excluding:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: only one entrance to internal parking located above or below the ground floor, (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a portal,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency exit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an entrance to a room dedicated to a solid waste management area; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: not exceed a maximum width of 24.0 metres for each premise facing a streetline; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: contain an independent pedestrian entrance for each premise facing a streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subsection 144(1) shall not apply to:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a change of use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a low-density dwelling use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a religious institution use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency services use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a cultural use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a shared housing with special care use. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: For any addition to any main building, the requirement of Subsection 144(1) shall only apply to the length of the streetwall addition.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 144(2) and 144(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Grade-oriented premises shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline. Grade-Related Dwelling Units

sections.title_label_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of
provisions.text: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of one exterior pedestrian entrance. Minimum Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: Any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall be

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall be
provisions.text: Any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall be separated by a minimum required distance of 23.0 metres from any portions of the same or other main buildings on the same lot that are above 33.5 metres in height. Maximum Building Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 154, any portion of any main building above

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 154, any portion of any main building above
provisions.text: Subject to Section 154, any portion of any main building above 33.5 metres in height shall not exceed the following maximum building dimension requirements of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 154, any portion of any main building above
provisions.text: within the Downtown Halifax Central Blocks (DHCB) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 154, any portion of any main building above
provisions.text: a building depth of 27.5 metres, and

provisions.text: For the purpose of measuring building dimensions in Subsection 147(1), main buildings connected by a pedway shall be measured separately, excluding the pedway. Accessory Structures and Shipping Containers

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures and shipping containers shall meet the

provisions.text: Accessory structures and shipping containers shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
